'''James Roumanos''' ([[Arabic language|Arabic]]: جيمس رومانوس) (born 10 August 1999) is a [[Lebanon national rugby league team|Lebanon international]] [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|lf}} or {{rlp|pr}} for [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]] in the [[National Rugby League]].

==Career==
===Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs===
On 31 August 2021, he was one of twelve players who were told by Canterbury that they would not be offered a contract for the 2022 season and would be released at seasons end.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2021-bulldogs-confirm-departing-players-dylan-napa-lachlan-lewis-will-hopoate-roster-contracts/news-story/0f92571846a3b360fa3df2cd52db5dd8/|title=Bulldogs show Origin star the door as Gus puts broom through Belmore|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>

===Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles===
In round 25 of the [[2022 NRL season]], Roumanos made his first grade debut off the bench for the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] against the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] in a 21-20 loss. He made 3 runs for 39 metres and 8 tackles in 9 minutes.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-bulldogs-vs-manly-sea-eagles-live-updates-live-stream-teams-start-time-supercoach-scores-video/news-story/0ae36ca01dea2c078c61da58eecbc829/|title=Bulldogs finally show ‘guts’ as TPJ sparks revival; Foran’s farewell spoiled by late FG: 3 Big Hits|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>
===Wests Tigers===
He signed a contract for [[Wests Tigers]] for the 2023 [[NRL]] season.{{fact|date=January 2024}}

===Toulouse Olympique===
On 18 November 2023 it was reported that he had signed for [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a one-year deal for the 2024 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total Rugby League|date=18 Nov 2023|title=Toulouse Olympique sign Castleford Tigers target|url=https://www.totalrl.com/toulouse-olympique-sign-castleford-tigers-target/}}</ref>

==International==
Roumanos made his international debut for [[Lebanon national rugby league team|Lebanon]] in their 56-14 loss to [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] in the 2019 Pacific Test.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/fiji-v-lebanon-international-2019/719011#lineup |title=Fiji vs Lebanon |publisher=Wide World of Sports |date=22 June 2019}}</ref>
